text: Fill in the blanks about muscle tissue histology
questions: Skeletal muscle fibres have *multiple/many* nuclei located at the *periphery/peripheral* position beneath the sarcolemma.
Cardiac muscle has *central/centrally placed* nuclei, visible cross-striations, and unique *intercalated discs/intercalated* at cell-to-cell junctions.
Smooth muscle cells are *spindle/fusiform* shaped with a *single/one* central elongated nucleus and no striations.
The basic contractile unit of striated muscle is the *sarcomere/sarcomeres*, bounded by *Z lines/Z discs* on either side.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the topic and try again.
